message:
fix(web/rendering): add missing v_uv varying input to CRT fragment shader

The CRT post-processing fragment shader was missing the `in vec2 v_uv;`
varying declaration that receives the UV coordinates from the vertex shader.
While some WebGL implementations may have been tolerating this by implicitly
linking the varying, this is technically invalid GLSL ES 3.0 and could cause:

- Shader compilation failures on stricter GPU drivers
- Undefined behavior when sampling textures at incorrect coordinates
- Visual artifacts in the CRT scanline/curvature effects

The vertex shader already outputs `out vec2 v_uv;` but the fragment shader
was missing the corresponding `in vec2 v_uv;` declaration. This fix adds
the proper varying input to ensure correct UV coordinate interpolation
across the full-screen quad used for post-processing.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
